fault_hypothesis: The function incorrectly removes the last element from the merged list if both input lists share the same last element. This is caused by the conditional check on line 76 which leads to an erroneous pop() on line 77.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left__1 and right__1 and merged__1[-1] == left__1[-1] == right__1[-1])

constraints.intent: The special case for handling common tail elements should never be triggered, as it leads to incorrect results.
The final merged list must contain all elements from both inputs.
After this branch, the total count of elements must still be preserved, but it is not.
After extending with both lists, the merged list should have the combined length.
